story_clusters.summary: India plans to launch a new Producer Price Index (PPI) as part of a broader data revamp to measure inflation more comprehensively. The government aims to phase out the existing Wholesale Price Index (WPI) over five years and introduce the PPI as a replacement. The Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT) will unveil a new WPI series alongside the PPI initiative.

Reports indicate a potential split in the TMC as most MLAs rally behind an expelled legislator for the position of Leader of the Opposition. Additionally, the party faces issues such as absent MLAs, expulsions, and a protest call without police permission.

perspectives.unique_angle: RBI June MPC decision: When and where to watch Guv Malhotra's address RBI June MPC decision: When and where to watch Guv Malhotra's address    Business Standard

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) meeting is underway, with economists and analysts debating whether the central bank will maintain its current policy stance amid concerns over inflation driven by the Middle East conflict. Multiple reports suggest the RBI is unlikely to hike rates in the near term, with Morgan Stanley stating there is no case for rate hikes at present, while some economists anticipate a status quo in June and potential hikes in FY27. Inflation and rupee weakness remain key factors influencing the RBI’s decision.

This initiative aims to improve water supply infrastructure and ensure last-mile delivery of tap water connections to rural households. articles.title: Green Competition: Why India's Competition Act Must Account For Environment
articles.summary: India has transformed how it thinks about corporate wrongdoing. The Jan Vishwas (Amendment of Provisions) Act,2023 decriminalized over 183 provisions across 42 Central Acts, converting criminal penalties into civil ones with enhanced monetary fines. The aim is straightforward, ease of doing buisness, not jail terms should be the default response to corporate non-compliance. The Competition (Amendment) Act, 2023 reflects the same instinct with heavier penalties, faster process but no prison bars

articles.title: Arbitration | Limitation For S. 34 Appeal Runs From Disposal Of S.33 Application Whether Allowed Or Rejected: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the limitation period for challenging an arbitral award under Section 34 of the Arbitration and Conciliation Act begins only after the disposal of a Section 33 application (for correction, interpretation, or supplementation of the award). The court clarified that once a Section 33 application is entertained, the award remains subject to the tribunal's limited jurisdiction. This ruling impacts how parties time their appeals against arbitral awards in India.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court on Tuesday (June 2) reiterated that the limitation period under Section 34(3) of the Act for filing an application seeking setting aside of an arbitral award would begin from the date on which a Section 33 application is disposed of. “Once proceedings under Section 33 are initiated and entertained by the Arbitral Tribunal, the award remains subject to the limited jurisdiction of the tribunal for correction, interpretation, or supplementation as contemplated under the provision.

This decision sets aside the conviction and sentence in the case, highlighting the court's authority to intervene in such matters.

articles.title: 'No Constitutional Protection For Betting On Games Of Skill' : Supreme Court Upholds TN, Karnataka Laws Banning Online Games With Stakes
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that wagering on games of skill does not enjoy constitutional protection under Article 19, upholding laws in Tamil Nadu and Karnataka that ban online games involving real-money stakes. The judgment clarifies that while games of skill themselves may be protected, betting on them falls outside this safeguard. This decision reinforces state-level restrictions on online gambling platforms and has implications for the broader regulatory landscape of digital gaming in India.
articles.raw_summary: While games of skill may be protected by Article 19, wagering on games of skill does not enjoy such protection, the Court held.

articles.title: Accused Entitled To Default Bail If Final Report E-Filed After 5 PM On Last Day Of Prescribed Statutory Period: Kerala High Court
articles.summary: The Kerala High Court recently held that an accused would be entitled to default bail if the final report was e-filed by the police after 5 p.m. on the last day of the statutory period prescribed. The Court's reasoning was that since as per Electronic Filing Rules for Courts (Kerala), 2021, e-filings made after 5 pm are deemed to have been instituted on the next working day, the final report would be deemed to have been filed on the day after the statutory period. The decision follows intervention by the regulator, and the matter will be revisited after the Uttar Pradesh Power Corporation Limited (UPPCL) submits its response. The proposed surcharge would have directly impacted household electricity costs in the state.

articles.title: SC: Criminal cases on loan defaulters must end after settlement
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that criminal cases against loan defaulters must end if they reach a settlement with banks. The court stated that pursuing such cases after a compromise is oppressive and harms judicial processes and the economy. This decision underscores the sanctity of settlements and aims to encourage dispute resolution in financial matters.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that criminal proceedings against loan defaulters must cease if a settlement is reached with the bank. Such actions, the court stated, are oppressive and abuse judicial processes, potentially harming the economy. Allowing banks to pursue criminal cases after a compromise undermines settlement sanctity and discourages dispute resolution.

articles.title: Insolvency recoveries nearly halve in FY26 due to rising delays: Report
articles.summary: The Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) saw recoveries under admitted claims nearly halve to 23% in FY2025-26 from 46% the previous year due to rising delays. The report, citing these figures, suggests the slowdown in recoveries could impact the effectiveness of the IBC, a critical legal tool for resolving corporate insolvencies in India.

articles.title: India's Import Duties: Mills Win This Round as Farmers Absorb the Cotton Risk. Once Again
articles.summary: Cotton is the raw material on which India's textile and apparel export ambitions rest, and for the second time in a year the government has moved to lower its cost for processors by removing import duties. What it does not do is resolve the underlying tension between farm-income protection and industrial competitiveness that has made the duty a recurring site of policy conflict.

articles.title: Supreme Court Collegium recommends elevation of four judges, senior advocate for top court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court Collegium has recommended the elevation of four judges and a senior advocate, including woman senior advocate V Mohana, to the top court. This comes at a time when the Supreme Court has only one woman judge, Justice Nagarathna, and no woman has been appointed since August 2021. The move addresses concerns about gender representation in the judiciary and may influence future policy discussions on judicial diversity and inclusion.

The project reflects a global race for quantum supremacy and could shape India's future in advanced technology. The initiative may prompt further state or central government policy actions in deep technology and innovation.

Can Chandrababu Naidu, the state’s chief minister, turn raw talent into commercial power?

articles.title: ASCI releases draft synthetic media guidelines for ads, certain uses to be prohibited entirely
articles.summary: The Advertising Standards Council of India (ASCI) has proposed draft guidelines for AI-generated advertising, requiring disclosures for high-risk synthetic content while banning deceptive uses entirely. The framework aligns with the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology's (MeitY) stricter synthetic media compliance rules. This move aims to address the risks of AI-generated ads in misleading consumers.
articles.raw_summary: ASCI has proposed risk-based guidelines for AI-generated advertising, requiring disclosures for high-risk synthetic content while banning deceptive AI uses. The framework complements MeitY’s stricter synthetic media compliance rules. The post ASCI releases draft synthetic media guidelines for ads, certain uses to be prohibited entirely appeared first on MEDIANAMA .

articles.title: Kerala launches statewide cyber safety pledge for students on school reopening day
articles.summary: Kerala Police launched the 'Kid Glove Project' on June 1, when schools reopened, requiring students to take a cyber safety pledge. The initiative aims to educate young internet users about online threats, cyber hygiene, privacy protection, fake news, and responsible digital behavior. The project is part of Kerala's efforts to address growing concerns about digital risks among schoolchildren.
articles.raw_summary: As schools reopen across Kerala on June 1, students will take a cyber safety pledge under the Kerala Police's Kid Glove Project. The initiative seeks to raise awareness about online threats, cyber hygiene, privacy protection, fake news and responsible digital behaviour among young internet users.

articles.summary: New digital marking system is aimed at reducing human errors but many students say it has resulted in wrong grades National outcry has erupted in India after more than 400,000 students have requested copies of their exam papers and answer sheets amid an outcry over marking errors in the country’s most important school-leaving exams. The coalition aims to improve customer experiences, support small businesses and delivery partners, and promote economic growth through collective efforts. It will also serve as a platform for responsible and sustainable growth in the digital commerce sector.

articles.summary: The US is considering expanding the deployment of nuclear-capable bombers across additional European countries. articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that marriage does not sever a daughter's ties with her parental family, asserting that gender stereotypes incompatible with constitutional principles cannot be upheld. This ruling addresses gender discrimination and reinforces constitutional rights, particularly for women.

articles.summary: The NSUI, the student wing of the Congress party, has filed a Public Interest Litigation (PIL) in court regarding glitches in the CBSE's Online Scrutiny Mechanism (OSM) for answer sheet verification. The PIL highlights concerns about the integrity of the examination process and seeks accountability from the education regulator.

The case involved long-standing legal ambiguities over the rights of former royal families to their ancestral properties.

The decision highlights challenges in India's aviation sector linked to geopolitical tensions and economic factors.
articles.raw_summary: IndiGo is temporarily halting its wide-body flights to Manchester from August 31 due to extended flight routes caused by airspace restrictions amid the Iran conflict. Activists and villagers allege lack of proper consent and demand policy intervention.

More than 200 police personnel had encircled the village, say the residents. [ ]

articles.title: Married daughter entitled to compassionate job: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that married daughters are entitled to be considered part of a 'family' for employment or ration shop allotment if their parents die while still working. The court struck down a Uttar Pradesh government order that excluded married daughters from this definition, calling it unconstitutional for perpetuating gender stereotypes. The ruling reinforces the principle of equality under the law. The decision applies to cases where parents die 'in harness' (while still employed).
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court has ruled that married daughters are entitled to be considered part of a 'family' for employment or ration shop allotment if their parents die in harness. The court found a UP government order excluding married daughters from the definition of 'family' unconstitutional, citing gender-based stereotypes and the principle of equality.

The warning underscores the evolving threat landscape in cybersecurity.

articles.title: Chana prices climb over 9% in a month
articles.summary: Chana (chickpea) prices in India have increased by over 9% in a month despite a strong harvest and large government buffer stocks. Government procurement is absorbing much of the market supply, while lower imports of chana and yellow peas are also contributing to the price rise. Traders expect prices to remain firm in the near term.

articles.title: States asked to focus on sowing pulses, oilseeds
articles.summary: India's government is urging states to focus on pulses and oilseeds for the upcoming kharif sowing season. This directive comes amid predictions of below-normal monsoon rains and potential disruptions in fertilizer supplies. The aim is to boost domestic production and reduce reliance on imports. Efforts are underway to improve seed availability and farming techniques for these crucial crops.

articles.title: New producer price index set to replace WPI over next five years
articles.summary: India is set to introduce a new Producer Price Index, covering output, input, and services. This move aims to provide a more accurate picture of inflation. The existing Wholesale Price Index will be phased out over five years. The new indices will use 2022-23 as the base year. This transition aligns with global standards and IMF recommendations.

articles.summary: India is set to introduce a new Producer Price Index and a revamped Wholesale Price Index from June 15. New Producer Price Indices for goods and services will also be rolled out.

articles.title: Extreme heat tests India's record milk production
articles.summary: India's booming dairy sector, a global leader, faces a significant threat from escalating heatwaves. Farmers like Neeraj Bharadwaj are witnessing premature births and reduced milk yields, forcing costly adaptations. While research into heat-resilient breeds and improved farm practices is underway, smallholders struggle to afford these changes, jeopardizing future milk supply amidst growing demand.

articles.summary: India's edible oil imports increased in May, driven by a surge in soyoil purchases. Palm oil imports saw a slight rise but remained below average. articles.title: A ₹3 lakh crore rain check? India’s monsoon now comes with a Hormuz fine print
articles.summary: India’s fertiliser supply chain is under strain as the West Asia conflict disrupts shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical route for imports. The crisis comes just as India faces a below-normal monsoon forecast, with the IMD projecting rainfall at 90% of the long-term average and a 92% probability of El Niño conditions, raising concerns over crop output and food inflation.

articles.summary: Forecasts indicate that by FY27, India’s inflation could hit 4.8%, driven by crude oil prices projected at USD 90 per barrel. Factors such as the ongoing conflict in West Asia and a less favorable monsoon outlook are pivotal. Economic growth might taper to 6.3%, with both fiscal and current account deficits expected to increase.

articles.title: Cotton prices down 3% on import duty suspension, may slide more
articles.summary: India has suspended its 11% import duty on cotton until October. This move has immediately reversed the recent rally in cotton prices, with both bales and yarn seeing a drop. This event is seen as a boost to India's tiger conservation efforts under existing wildlife protection policies.

articles.title: India’s power paradox: Why power prices crashed even as India’s electricity demand surged
articles.summary: The challenge ahead is no longer solely about adding gigawatts, but about ensuring they are priced, dispatched and consumed intelligently. articles.title: Right to trauma care of citizens integral part of right to life: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India ruled that the right to trauma care is an integral part of the right to life under Article 21 of the Constitution. The court emphasized that trauma care is critical in emergencies like accidents, where victims often experience shock and helplessness. This ruling underscores the state's obligation to ensure timely medical assistance. The decision has broad implications for healthcare policy and emergency services in the country.
articles.raw_summary: The top court said that when a person suffers an accident or any similar incident which requires urgent trauma care, they usually feel shock and disorientation, a sense of helplessness, where they have to hope that those around them will somehow help them get the care that they need.

articles.title: Strong El Nino developing, South Asia to get below normal rain, flags WMO
articles.summary: The World Meteorological Organization (WMO) has warned that a strong El Nino is developing, which could result in below-normal rainfall across much of South Asia during the June–September 2026 monsoon season. This forecast raises concerns about potential impacts on agriculture, water resources, and livelihoods in the region, including India. The WMO's alert highlights the need for preparedness and mitigation measures.
articles.raw_summary: Rainfall is likely to be below normal during the June–September 2026 southwest monsoon season across much of South Asia

articles.title: Pakistan's Islamabad orders markets to shut by 8 pm amid energy crisis due to US-Iran war
articles.summary: Pakistan has ordered markets to close by 8 pm to conserve energy amid a fuel shortage caused by the US-Iran war. The conflict has driven up global energy prices, increasing inflation and straining fuel-importing countries across Asia. Higher fuel costs could impact India's energy security and economic policies.
articles.raw_summary: The conflict has increased pressure on fuel-importing countries across Asia. Higher energy prices are driving up inflation and straining external finances.

articles.title: Seas, mountains, deserts: How Iran's geography acts as its ultimate shield
articles.summary: Iran's geography, including mountain ranges, deserts, and coastlines along the Caspian Sea, Persian Gulf, and Sea of Oman, plays a critical role in its defense and global oil trade. The Strait of Hormuz, a key chokepoint for oil shipments, is highlighted as a vital artery for global energy supplies. This geographic advantage has significant implications for India's energy imports and regional geopolitical dynamics.
articles.raw_summary: Iran has two major mountain ranges, deserts and coastlines along the Caspian Sea, Persian Gulf and Sea of Oman. The Strait of Hormuz is a key artery for oil.

articles.title: Mint Explainer: Why India wants to go back to standard edible oil pack sizes
articles.summary: The government plans to bring back standard pack sizes for edible oils on complaints that similar-looking pouches with different quantities and prices were making value-for-money comparisons difficult for buyers. The move aims to improve transparency and ease shopping choices.
